title: senecio lactucinus
intro: Senecio lactucinus, also known as Lactuce or Wild Lettuce, is a perennial plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. Its leaves, stems, and flowers are used in herbal medicine, known for their expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including bronchitis, coughs, and respiratory infections, due to its ability to thin mucus and reduce inflammation. The leaves can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to promote relaxation and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
